pending_diagnosis: DIAGNOSIS: Anti-NMDA Receptor Encephalitis THE SMOKING GUN: A 4cm mature cystic ovarian teratoma discovered on pelvic imaging PATHOPHYSIOLOGY: This condition is an autoimmune encephalitis where the body produces antibodies against the GluN1 subunit of the N-methyl-D-aspartate (NMDA) receptor. In approximately half of female cases, the trigger is a paraneoplastic response to an ovarian teratoma. The tumor contains nervous tissue that expresses NMDA receptors, prompting the immune system to create antibodies that then cross-react with receptors in the patient's own brain. This leads to a massive internalization of receptors, causing the classic clinical progression from psychiatric symptoms to seizures, orofacial dyskinesias, and life-threatening autonomic instability.
